data.content:
Kubernetes
Kubernetes is an open-source container orchestration system for automating deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications.<SEP>Kubernetes is an open-source container orchestration system; the text describes its garbage collection behavior.<SEP>Kubernetes is an open-source container orchestration system.<SEP>Kubernetes is an open-source container orchestration system.<SEP>Kubernetes is an open-source container orchestration system.  The text discusses its use with Ingress controllers and the Gateway API.

kube-state-metrics
kube-state-metrics is a Kubernetes add-on that exposes metrics about the state of Kubernetes objects.

Prometheus
Prometheus is an open-source monitoring and alerting system that can be used to collect and analyze metrics from kube-state-metrics.

kubectl
Kubectl is the command-line tool for Kubernetes, used to manage and interact with Kubernetes clusters.<SEP>kubectl is the command-line tool for interacting with Kubernetes clusters.

Pod
A Pod is a group of one or more containers that are deployed together in Kubernetes.<SEP>A Pod is a group of one or more containers that are deployed together on a Kubernetes cluster.

Container
A Container is a standardized unit of software that packages code and all its dependencies so the application runs quickly and reliably from one computing environment to another.<SEP>A container is a standardized unit of software that packages code and all its dependencies so the application runs quickly and reliably from one computing environment to another.

kube_pod_container_info
A metric exposed by kube-state-metrics providing information about containers within pods.

kube_pod_status_readycondition
A metric from kube-state-metrics indicating the readiness status of pods.

kube_pod_deletion_timestamp
A metric from kube-state-metrics showing the timestamp of pod deletion.

Standard Output (stdout)
Standard Output (stdout) is one of the two standard streams used by programs to send data to the console or other output destinations.  It typically displays normal program output.<SEP>Standard output stream for a container, used for logging.

Standard Error (stderr)
Standard Error (stderr) is a standard stream used by programs to report errors or diagnostic messages.  It is separate from standard output to allow for easier error handling and monitoring.<SEP>Standard error stream for a container, used for logging errors.

PodLogsQuerySplitStreams
A Kubernetes feature gate enabling separate logging of stdout and stderr from pods.<SEP>PodLogsQuerySplitStreams is a feature gate in Kubernetes that enables the splitting of container logs into standard output and standard error streams.

Node
A Node represents a worker machine in a Kubernetes cluster.

Cluster-level logging
A logging architecture where logs are stored and managed separately from nodes, pods, and containers.

kubelet
The kubelet is a component of the Kubernetes system responsible for managing containers on a node.<SEP>The kubelet is the agent that runs on each node in a Kubernetes cluster.<SEP>kubelet is the agent that runs on each node in a Kubernetes cluster.

ReplicaSets
ReplicaSets ensure that a specified number of pod replicas are running at any given time.

DaemonSets
DaemonSets ensure that all nodes in a cluster run a copy of a pod.

Deployments
Deployments manage the desired state of a set of pods.

Jobs
Jobs create one or more pods and ensure that a specified number of them successfully terminate.

CronJobs
CronJobs create Jobs on a schedule.

ReplicationControllers
ReplicationControllers ensure that a specified number of pod replicas are running at any given time (deprecated in favor of ReplicaSets).

Pods
Pods are the smallest and simplest units in the Kubernetes object model that you create or deploy.

Service
A Service is an abstraction which defines a logical set of Pods and a policy by which to access them.

EndpointSlice
EndpointSlice is a Kubernetes API object that represents a subset of endpoints for a service.

Imperative Object Configuration
A method of Kubernetes object configuration where the user explicitly defines the operations to be performed on objects.  It's best suited for files, not directories, and changes to live objects may be lost if not reflected in configuration files.

Declarative Object Configuration
A method of Kubernetes object configuration where the user defines the desired state of objects, and Kubernetes automatically determines the necessary operations (create, update, delete). It supports operations on directories and retains changes made by other writers.

configs directory
A directory containing Kubernetes object configuration files.

patch API operation
A Kubernetes API operation that applies only the differences between the current and desired state of an object.

replace API operation
A Kubernetes API operation that replaces the entire object configuration with a new one.

Owner
An Owner is an object in a system (likely Kubernetes) upon which a Dependent relies. The presence or absence of the Owner affects the Dependent's lifecycle.<SEP>In Kubernetes, an object that has ownership over other objects (dependents).

Dependent
A Dependent is an object in a system (likely Kubernetes) that relies on an Owner for its existence.  If the Owner is deleted, the Dependent may also be deleted.<SEP>In Kubernetes, an object that is owned by another object (owner).

metadata.ownerReferences field
A field in Kubernetes object metadata that specifies the owner of the object.

ownerReferences.blockOwnerDeletion field
A field in Kubernetes object metadata that controls whether a dependent object can block deletion of its owner.

Kubernetes admission controller
A Kubernetes component that controls user access to modify ownerReferences and blockOwnerDeletion fields.

labels and selectors
A mechanism in Kubernetes for selecting objects based on labels.

v1.20
Version 1.20 of a system (likely Kubernetes) introduced a change in how cluster-scoped dependents handle namespaced owners.

Namespace
A Namespace is a scoping mechanism, likely within Kubernetes, that organizes and isolates resources.

Cluster-scoped Dependent
A type of Dependent that is scoped to the entire cluster, not a specific namespace.

Cluster-scoped Owner
A type of Owner that is scoped to the entire cluster, not a specific namespace.

Namespaced Kind
A type of object within a system (likely Kubernetes) that is associated with a specific namespace.

Garbage Collection
The process of automatically removing objects that are no longer needed or referenced.

CSI driver
A CSI driver manages storage for container storage interface.  It handles operations like provisioning, deleting, attaching, detaching, mounting, unmounting, and resizing volumes.<SEP>A Container Storage Interface (CSI) driver is a software component that allows Kubernetes to interact with various storage systems.

kube-apiserver
The kube-apiserver is the main control plane component of Kubernetes.

PersistentVolume
A PersistentVolume (PV) represents a piece of storage in the cluster.

PersistentVolumeClaim
A PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC) is a request for storage by a pod.

Storage Class
A StorageClass defines a set of parameters for creating PVs.

in-tree plugin
An in-tree plugin is a storage plugin that is built into the Kubernetes codebase.

CSI node plugin
A CSI node plugin is a component that runs on each node and handles volume operations.

csi-proxy
csi-proxy is a community-managed binary used for privileged operations on Windows nodes for containerized CSI node plugins.

Windows
Windows is an operating system.

Linux
Linux is an operating system.

CreateVolumeResponse
A Kubernetes API response related to volume creation.

CSI spec
The Container Storage Interface specification.

ControllerPublishVolumeRequest
A Kubernetes API request to publish a volume to a controller.

NodeStageVolumeRequest
A Kubernetes API request to stage a volume on a node.

NodePublishVolumeRequest
A Kubernetes API request to publish a volume on a node.

ControllerPublishVolume
A CSI method to publish a volume to a controller.

ControllerUnpublishVolume
A CSI method to unpublish a volume from a controller.

NodeExpandVolume
A CSI method to expand a volume on a node.

NodeStageVolume
A CSI method to stage a volume on a node.

NodePublishVolume
A CSI method to publish a volume on a node.

CSINodeExpandSecret
A Kubernetes feature gate enabling secret passing for node volume expansion.

PersistentVolumePersistentVolumeClaim
Refers to the combined use of PersistentVolumes and PersistentVolumeClaims.

Ephemeral Volumes
Volumes that do not persist across pod restarts.

kubernetes-csi documentation
Documentation for developing CSI drivers.

CSIMigration
A Kubernetes feature for migrating from in-tree plugins to CSI drivers.<SEP>CSIMigration is a process or feature related to migrating to or using the Container Storage Interface (CSI). In-tree plugins supporting this are mentioned.

PV
PV refers to Persistent Volumes, a type of storage in Kubernetes.  The text indicates that PV creation continues to work with CSI drivers.

In-tree plugins
These are plugins integrated directly into the system's codebase.  The text mentions that those supporting CSIMigration and having a corresponding CSI driver are listed elsewhere.

NodeRestriction
NodeRestriction is a Kubernetes admission plugin that prevents modification of labels with a specific prefix.

Node authorizer
The Node authorizer is a component of Kubernetes responsible for authorizing node operations.

Labels
Labels are key-value pairs used to annotate Kubernetes objects, including Nodes and Pods.

nodeSelector
nodeSelector is a mechanism in Kubernetes to constrain Pod scheduling based on Node labels.

Affinity
Affinity is a Kubernetes feature that allows for more complex Pod scheduling constraints based on Node and Pod labels.

Anti-affinity
Anti-affinity is a Kubernetes feature that prevents co-location of Pods based on labels.

Node Affinity
A Kubernetes feature that allows pods to be scheduled only on nodes with specific labels, improving resource allocation and application deployment.<SEP>Node Affinity is a type of affinity that constrains Pod scheduling based on Node labels.

Inter-pod Affinity
Inter-pod Affinity is a type of affinity that constrains Pod scheduling based on labels of other Pods.

requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution
A type of Node Affinity rule where the scheduler must find a matching Node; the rule is ignored after scheduling.

preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution
A type of Node Affinity rule where the scheduler prefers a matching Node but will schedule elsewhere if necessary; the rule is ignored after scheduling.

topology.kubernetes.io/zone
A Kubernetes label indicating the zone or region of a Node.

another-node-label-key
Example of a custom node label key.

another-node-label-value
Example of a custom node label value.

data.entity_name: In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist, Gt, Lt
data.content:
In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist, Gt, Lt
Logical operators used in Kubernetes selectors.

In
A logical operator used in Kubernetes for node affinity rules.  Indicates that a node must have a specific label.

NotIn
A logical operator used in Kubernetes for node affinity rules. Indicates that a node must NOT have a specific label.

Exists
A logical operator used in Kubernetes for node affinity rules. Indicates that a specific label must exist on a node.

DoesNotExist
A logical operator used in Kubernetes for node affinity rules. Indicates that a specific label must NOT exist on a node.

Gt
A logical operator used in Kubernetes for node affinity rules. Indicates that a numerical label value must be greater than a specified value.

Lt
A logical operator used in Kubernetes for node affinity rules. Indicates that a numerical label value must be less than a specified value.

Node Anti-affinity
A Kubernetes feature that prevents pods from being scheduled on the same node, enhancing availability and fault tolerance.

Gateway API
The Gateway API is a Kubernetes API specification for managing ingress resources. It's presented as an alternative to traditional Ingress controllers.

AWS
Amazon Web Services provides an Ingress controller supported by Kubernetes.

GCE
Google Compute Engine provides an Ingress controller supported by Kubernetes.

nginx
Nginx provides an Ingress controller supported by Kubernetes.

AKS Application Gateway Ingress Controller
An Ingress controller that configures the Azure Application Gateway.

Azure Application Gateway
A service offered by Azure for managing ingress traffic.

Alibaba Cloud MSE Ingress
An Ingress controller that configures the Alibaba Cloud Native Gateway.

Alibaba Cloud Native Gateway
A service offered by Alibaba Cloud for managing ingress traffic; commercial version of Higress.

Higress
An open-source ingress controller.

Apache APISIX ingress controller
An Ingress controller based on Apache APISIX.

Apache APISIX
An open-source API gateway.

Avi Kubernetes Operator
Provides L4-L7 load-balancing using VMware NSX Advanced Load Balancer.

VMware NSX Advanced Load Balancer
A load balancer from VMware.

BFE Ingress Controller
An Ingress controller based on BFE.

BFE
A company that provides a network infrastructure solution, including an Ingress controller.

Cilium Ingress Controller
An Ingress controller powered by Cilium.

Cilium
A company that provides a networking and security solution for Kubernetes.

Citrix ingress controller
Works with Citrix Application Delivery Controller.

Contour
An Envoy-based Ingress controller.

Envoy
A high-performance proxy.<SEP>Envoy is an API gateway that can function as an ingress controller.  It is mentioned in relation to another API gateway, suggesting a comparison or contrast in functionality.

Emissary-Ingress API Gateway
An Envoy-based Ingress controller.

EnRoute
An Envoy-based API gateway that can run as an Ingress controller.

Easegress IngressController
An Easegress-based API gateway that can run as an Ingress controller.<SEP>This is a specific implementation or product of Easegress, designed to function as a Kubernetes ingress controller.

Easegress
An API gateway.<SEP>Easegress is an API gateway that can function as an ingress controller. It is mentioned in relation to Envoy, suggesting a comparison or contrast in functionality.

Ingress
Ingress is a Kubernetes resource for routing external traffic to services within a cluster.  The document discusses its functionality and the need for an Ingress controller.

Ingress Controller
An Ingress controller is a component that implements the Ingress resource, routing external traffic to services in a Kubernetes cluster.

Citrix Application Delivery Controller
A Citrix product for application delivery.

Custom Resources
Custom Resources are extensions to the Kubernetes API.

Implementations
Different implementations of the Gateway API.

httpsgetenroute.io
This is a URL likely pointing to an organization or a product.  The context suggests it's related to an Envoy-based API gateway.

Envoy-based API gateway
This refers to an API gateway built using Envoy technology.

ingress controller
An ingress controller is a component that manages external access to services in a Kubernetes cluster.

httpswww.envoyproxy.io
This URL likely points to the official website of the Envoy project.

httpsmegaease.comdocseasegress04.cloud-native4.1.kubernetes-ingress-controller
This URL likely points to documentation for the Easegress Ingress Controller.

httpsmegaease.comeasegress
This URL likely points to the official website of the Easegress project.
